creator: rea@FreeBSD.org
text:
Some concerns:

 - you should not set LATEST_LINK -- this is a beta port that isn't
   supposed to show up on the 'Latest' package list;

 - port name should be better 'dovecot2' -- just now your port is
   restricted to 2.2.x; what will you do when Dovecot will be at
   2.3.<something>?  Dovecot 1.x and 2.x are sufficiently different to
   allow for different ports for them, but I am not sure for Dovecot
   2.2, 2.3, etc; though it is only my view on this.

 - you should set DISTNAME; otherwise 'make fetch' won't work; probably,
   you should also use PKGNAMESUFFIX for "-devel";

 - the comment block about PORTVERSION bump looks suspicious; I think
   that the dovecot2-pigeonhole should take into account the presence
   of this new development port (if the comment part about breakage
   is right) or, probably, this comment isn't relevant and should be
   deleted;

 - probably, you should talk to the maintainer of mail/dovecot2 and
   make a strategy of taking the good changes from each other;  does
   mail/dovecot2-devel is needed for you to test the new features
   of Dovecot, or to run "stable" development releases in production
   because they have some needed features, or for anything else?

 - please, build you port from the clean state (wiped distfiles,
   clean port directory) and use portlint and 'port test' from
   ports-mgmt/porttools.
-- 
Eygene Ryabinkin                                        ,,,^..^,,,
[ Life's unfair - but root password helps!           | codelabs.ru ]
